stories.title: Janša closes in on fourth stint as Slovenia’s prime minister
stories.summary: Slovenian lawmakers on Tuesday proposed nationalist leader Janez Janša become the country’s prime minister, putting the veteran conservative on track to return to power for a fourth time after his party narrowly lost national elections in March. Janša announced his intention to stand as a candidate to lead the country’s next government on Monday after the leadership of his Slovenian Democratic Party (SDS) green-lighted a coalition agreement with several center-right parties. “With this, th

stories.title: NATO planes down suspected Ukrainian drone over Estonia
stories.summary: A NATO military jet has shot down a suspected Ukrainian drone in Estonian airspace. As Ukraine targets Russia's Baltic oil facilities more often and more intensely, more such drones are straying into allies' territory.

stories.title: ‘Everything worked’ says Nato commander after its jets shot down stray drone over Estonia – Europe live
stories.summary: Chief commander Alexus Grynkewich also confirms the withdrawal of 5,000 US troops from Europe in line with Trump’s plans The latest drone alerts come as Ukraine and Latvia were this morning forced by Russia to repeatedly refute Moscow’s claims that Kyiv was preparing attacks against Russia from Latvia. Ukraine’s foreign ministry spokesperson confirmed that Ukraine does not use the territory of Latvia for its operations against Russia and refuted Moscow’s claims. “ Russia is lying about Latvia al

stories.title: Ukraine war briefing: Chinese ship hit on way in to Odesa port, says Zelenskyy
stories.summary: Civilian vessels under Russian attack in Black Sea; Kyiv condemns Belarus nuclear drills as threat to Nato and global security. What we know on day 1,546 Continue reading...

stories.title: Sweden orders 4 frigates from French company Naval Group
stories.summary: Sweden plans to acquire four new frigates starting in 2030 in a deal worth billions. They will be the largest surface warships in Sweden's navy. The newest NATO member is rushing to build up its military capabilities.

stories.title: Czechia: Uproar over Sudeten German gathering in Brno
stories.summary: The first gathering of Sudeten Germans in Czechia since World War II is set to go ahead this weekend despite a political backlash that has exposed tensions over one of Central Europe's most painful historical legacies.

stories.title: Hungary's ambassador to Warsaw recalled ahead of Magyar delegation's visit
stories.summary: FM Anita Orbán said that Hungarian-Polish relations are entering a new era, and a new person will be better able to represent the renewed foreign policy direction with credibility and consistency in the period ahead.

stories.title: Nyilvánosan elérhetőek a kegyelmi ügy dokumentumai
stories.summary: A dokumentumok szerint Varga Judit nem javasolt kegyelmet K. Endrének, Novák Katalin azonban megváltoztatta a döntést. Az is kiderült, hogy Ferenc pápa budapesti látogatása miatt a Sándor-palota kérte az ügymenet felgyorsítását. A kormány szerint a magyar társadalomnak joga van megismerni, hogyan születhetett meg a döntés. Itt vannak a részletek.

stories.title: Arte: Kérdés, hogy mit tehet az EU az LMBTQ+ közösségért, ha Orbán és Fico eddig fittyet hányhatott a szabályokra
stories.summary: A szexuális kisebbségeket érő gyűlölet-bűncselekmények és erőszakos esetek egyre gyakoribbak.

stories.title: President urged expediting clemency case in 2023 citing upcoming papal visit
stories.summary: Part of the documents connected to one of the biggest scandals of the last few years in Hungary have now been released to the public, but it remains unclear why then president Novák chose to grant a presidential pardon – a decision which led to her resignation.

stories.title: Reagált az ÁSZ arra, hogy Magyar Péter 15-20 zsák ledarált iratot talált Lázár János volt minisztériumának épületében
stories.summary: A számvevőszék szerint a miniszterelnök téved, amikor arról beszél egy videón, hogy tiltott pártfinanszírozást is felvetnek a pincében talált iratok.

stories.title: Az MVM-csoport egy cége építheti a magyar–szerb olajvezetéket
stories.summary: Nem volt könnyű szülés, de végül kihirdették, hogy az egyetlen induló meg is nyerte a tendert.

stories.title: Az Orbán-rezsim egy hatalmas eltékozolt lehetőség: elköltöttünk közel 50 milliárd eurót arra, hogy hatalomban legyenek 16 évig – interjú Orbán Krisztiánnal
stories.summary: Milyen lesz az új világrend, amely a globalizáció eddig ismert formáját váltja fel? Hol lesz a helye ebben Magyarországnak, és milyen esélyekkel indul? Orbán Krisztián a globális hatalmi viszonyok átrendeződéséről.

stories.title: Magyar Péter keddtől miniszterelnök-helyettesnek nevezi ki Ruff Bálintot
stories.summary: Magyar Péter azt írta, „Orbán Anita miniszterelnök-helyettes és a saját egyidejű esetleges akadályoztatásom esetére” nevezi ki Ruffot.

stories.title: Lovász László szerint érthető, hogy a kormány felmondta az Élvonal Alapítvány szerződését
stories.summary: Az MTA volt elnöke az Élvonal kuratóriumának tagja, a Telexnek azt írta: nem elegendő az, hogy egy nagyszerű, Nobel-díjas tudóshoz rendelünk magyar léptékben szinte felfoghatatlan mértékű forrásokat.

stories.title: Magyar Péter arra utalt, Gaudi-Nagy Tamás írta K. Endre kegyelmi kérvényét, az ügyvéd szerint a kormányfő hazug uszító
stories.summary: Gaudi-Nagy érdemben nem reagált arra, hogy érintett-e a kegyelmi ügyben.

stories.title: Who's going to occupy Brussels now?
stories.summary: The billboards used to manipulate the masses over the past 16 years are gradually disappearing from Hungarian streets. The blue billboards, produced to spread fear of a non-existent threat of war may be gone, but they have left a lasting imprint on the psyche of an entire nation.

stories.title: Magyar Péter Gaudi-Nagy Tamásról: Ha hozzájárul, bemutatjuk a nevéhez köthető iratot. Ha van ilyen ¯\_(ツ)_/¯
stories.summary: Az ügyvéd arról, hogy köze lehet-e a kegyelmi ügyhöz, azt nyilatkozta, Magyar Péter „gyáva, narcisztikus és hazug uszító”.

stories.title: Orbán Krisztián az elszámoltatásról: Nem mindegy, hogy azt mondjuk, ő egy gazdag gazember, vagy egy gazdag ember, aki biztos ügyes volt
stories.summary: A közgazdász arról is beszélt a Telex élő adásában, hogy nem lehet megspórolni az elszámoltatást, ha nem azt akarjuk, hogy gazemberek szolgáljanak példaként a fiataloknak.

stories.title: Román vadászrepülő lőtt le egy ukrán drónt Észtországban
stories.summary: Riadót fújtak emiatt egy időre az ország déli részén.

stories.title: A lakossági állampapírokon is spórolni kezd a kormányzat
stories.summary: Kevesebbet lehet majd keresni a fix hozamú lakossági papírokkal, de ha valaki bízik a magyar euróbevezetés sikerében, akkor érdemes szétnéznie az egyéb állampapírok között.

stories.title: Kibékült Menczer Tamás és Bencsik András, együtt harcolnak tovább Magyarországért
stories.summary: Rendezték a sorokat.

stories.title: Zelenszkij a magyar kormánnyal folytatott párbeszédről: Számítok az eredményekre
stories.summary: Ukrajna elnöke szerint van esély az ukrán–magyar kapcsolatok konstruktív újraindítására.

stories.title: Hungary-Serbia oil pipeline to be built by company owned by Hungary's MVM Group
stories.summary: The Hungarian-Serbian consortium was the only one that had submitted a tender.

stories.title: Angela Merkel és Lech Wałęsa is az elsők között kapott Európai Érdemrendet
stories.summary: Összesen húszan kaptak elismerést, politikusok mellett akadnak köztük zenészek, kosárlabdázó és séf is.

stories.title: Tíz InterCity-kocsit kap kölcsön a MÁV nyárra az osztrák vasúttársaságtól, hogy ne dőljön be a balatoni közlekedés
stories.summary: Vitézy Dávid szerint ez „nem oldja meg a magyar vasút minden problémáját, de a következő hónapokban életmentő lehet”.

stories.title: Csökkent a hazai legális dohánypiac, de nem azért, mert mindenki leszokik
stories.summary: Állandó változásban a szektor, az adók nőnek, a legális fogyasztás apad, és évről évre belép a piacra valami újdonság. A hevített termékek után jöttek a nikotinpárnák és a vape-ek, csakhogy utóbbi nagyrészt illegális, mégis egyre inkább kiszorítja a hevített cigit.

stories.title: Zelensky "counting on results" in dialogue with Hungarian government
stories.summary: The Ukrainian President believes there are prospects for the constructive reset of the relationship between the two countries.

stories.title: Meghalt Scherer Péter
stories.summary: A Jászai Mari-díjas színművész 64 éves volt. A Karinthy Színház a magyar színházi élet pótolhatatlan alakjának nevezte Scherert, aki több tucat filmben és sorozatban is szerepelt.

stories.title: HVG: Vitézy Dávid kirúgta a MÁV gazdasági vezérigazgató-helyettesét
stories.summary: Eltávolítására utal, hogy a MÁV honlapján már nem Dányi neve szerepel a pozíciónál.

stories.title: „Lezárjuk az európai naivitás egy fejezetét” – szigorít az EU a külföldi befektetéseknél
stories.summary: Nagy többséggel támogatta az Európai Parlament a külföldi befektetések átvilágításának új szabályait, a magyar pártok háromféleképp szavaztak.

stories.title: Ruszin-Szendi Romulusz bejelentette az új vezérkari főnököt
stories.summary: Sándor Zsolt altábornagy azt a Böröndi Gábort váltja majd a poszton, aki Ruszin-Szendi Romulusz felmentése után lett vezérkari főnök.

stories.title: Régiós bajnok Magyarország az elektromobilitás-befektetésekben
stories.summary: Az elektromos autók és akkumulátorok gyártásába irányuló európai beruházások 11 százaléka valósult meg hazánkban.

stories.title: Xi prepares to welcome Putin to China four days after hosting Trump
stories.summary: Chinese state media say Beijing emerging as ‘focal point of global diplomacy’ with Russian leader arriving on Tuesday Xi Jinping and Vladimir Putin exchanged “congratulatory letters” on Sunday ahead of the Russian president’s visit to Beijing this week, four days after Donald Trump left China after a high-stakes summit . Xi said bilateral cooperation between Russia and China had “continuously deepened and solidified”, Chinese state media reported, with this year marking the 30th anniversary of t

stories.title: Could Romania and Moldova reunite?
stories.summary: Moldovan leader Maia Sandu and her Romanian counterpart Nicusor Dan want to reunite their countries. Is that a realistic scenario?

stories.title: Young Czechs drink less beer but risk other addictions
stories.summary: The Czech Republic, the beer world champion, has seen a record decline in consumption. Younger generations are drinking less. Breweries are worried, but health experts say it's good news.

stories.title: Latvia: Prime Minister Evika Silina resigns over handling of drone incidents after coalition ally's withdrawal
stories.summary: Evika Silina says she is resigning her post, following the defense minister's resignation and the coalition government's collapse amid controversy over Ukrainian drones aimed at Russia that landed in the Baltic country.

stories.summary: This blog is now closed Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Global Sumud Flotilla participants urge Australian government to intervene and ‘keep us safe’ The Australians that the Global Sumud Flotilla allege have been kidnapped by the Israel Defense Forces (IDF) are academics, doctors, students, activists and film-makers. stories.title: Bolivia: Clashes in La Paz as protesters demand President Paz's ouster
stories.summary: Riot police fired tear gas at demonstrators in Bolivia's capital, La Paz, while road blockades deepened shortages of fuel, food and medical supplies.

stories.title: New York federal judge bans ICE arrests at Manhattan immigration courts
stories.summary: Ruling blocks detentions at three federal courthouses except in rare cases amid protests over tactics A federal judge in New York has banned US Immigration and Customs Enforcement ( ICE ) agents from arresting immigrants in or around three federal courthouses in lower Manhattan, where vigorous confrontations have played out since the start of Donald Trump ’s second presidency. Under an order issued on Monday by P Kevin Castel, a US district judge, federal agents are no longer allowed to make arr

stories.title: As WHO sounds alarm over Ebola in DRC, what can be learned from previous outbreaks?
stories.summary: Conflict, mistrust and delayed detection could complicate response to emergency caused by Bundibugyo variant To be around the centre of an Ebola outbreak is to become used to the smell of chlorine. At hospitals and government buildings, surfaces are sprayed with it and hands washed in a 0.05% solution that can kill the virus in 60 seconds. Infrared handheld thermometers take temperatures at airports and border crossings. Any indication of a fever prevents passage. Contact-tracing teams crisscros

stories.title: Far-right Israeli minister Bezalel Smotrich says ICC is seeking his arrest
stories.summary: Smotrich vows to retaliate by waging ‘war’ on Palestinian Authority and orders evacuation of a West Bank village Middle East crisis – live updates Israel’s far-right finance minister, Bezalel Smotrich, has said the international criminal court (ICC) prosecutor has sought a confidential arrest warrant against him, and promised to retaliate by waging a “war” on the Palestinian Authority. He said he had ordered the evacuation of the Palestinian Bedouin village of Khan al-Ahmar in the Israeli-occupi

stories.title: Children’s reading should prioritise pleasure over learning, says laureate
stories.summary: Frank Cottrell-Boyce tells MPs to focus on early-years reading, with more support for parents and nursery workers The children’s laureate Frank Cottrell-Boyce has urged the government to prioritise pleasure over learning in children’s reading. Giving evidence to MPs on the education committee, which is investigating the crisis in reading for pleasure among children, the screenwriter and novelist said conversations about children’s reading too often revert to attainment in school. Continue readin

stories.title: Strike near UAE reactor revives concerns over nuclear plant safety in wartime
stories.summary: Attack marks first time military action has forced a fully operating nuclear power plant to rely on backup generators • Middle East crisis – live updates A drone strike that cut off external power to a nuclear reactor in the United Arab Emirates this week has revived concerns over the safety of nuclear plants during wartime. Reactor no 3 at the Barakah nuclear plant lost off-site power, which is critical for its own functioning, for about 24 hours after the attack on Sunday, forcing it to rely o

stories.title: Middle East crisis live: Iran warns it could ‘open new fronts’ against US as Trump repeats threats
stories.summary: Trump said the US may need to hit Iran again and he was only an hour away from deciding on a strike before he postponing Iran’s army has warned it would “open new fronts” against the US if it resumes attacks on the country amid reports that Donald Trump is weighing up restarting military operations in Iran amid an impasse in negotiations. “If the enemy is foolish enough to fall into the Zionist trap again and launches new aggression against our beloved Iran, we will open new fronts against it, w

stories.title: Moscow begins nuclear drills as Russia-Ukraine drone strikes intensify
stories.summary: As Russian and Ukrainian forces carried out fresh cross-border aerial strikes, Moscow launched nationwide nuclear drills.

stories.title: Oil prices slip as Trump calls off planned military strike on Iran
stories.summary: Oil prices fell on Tuesday as Donald Trump announced he was calling off a planned military attack on Iran.

stories.title: Anti-government demonstrators and police clash in Bolivia
stories.summary: Protesters have been calling for the president to resign after weeks-long unrest.

stories.title: Yes, Trump might really attack Cuba
stories.summary: The Trump administration has been dancing around the question of whether it will carry out military strikes against Cuba. I am told it is increasingly willing to take such a step. That’s a significant escalation from a few months ago, when officials were primarily focused on using economic and diplomatic pressure to squeeze the communist regime in Havana. A U.S. official and a person familiar with the administration’s discussions on Cuba told me that President Donald Trump and his aides have gro

stories.title: Belfast harbour operator to invest £1.3bn as NI economy grows
stories.summary: Port has upgraded offshore wind facilities and is to expand quays, ferry terminals and cruise ship services The operator of Belfast harbour plans to spend £1.3bn over the next 25 years to take advantage of strong economic growth in Northern Ireland, in what would be one of the largest non-governmental investments in the region’s history. The Belfast Harbour Commissioners said the money would be spent on upgrading the port, with the possibility of residential property developments that could add

stories.title: ‘Huge milestone’ as Libyan militia commander accused of torture appears at ICC
stories.summary: Prosecution is seen as landmark step towards justice over abuses of refugees trying to reach Europe from Africa A former militia commander accused of overseeing murder, rape, enslavement and torture in Libyan detention centres has appeared at the international criminal court for a hearing that campaigners say is a landmark step towards “justice, truth, reparation and deterrence” of abuses of refugees trying to reach Europe from Africa. The prosecution of Khaled Mohamed Ali El Hishri on charges o

stories.title: US claims ‘emergency refugee situation’ as it admits 10,000 more white South Africans
stories.summary: Trump has repeatedly made false claims that white Afrikaners facing genocide with costs of resettling them at $100m The US government has said it will increase the number of white South Africans it admits as refugees this year from about 7,500 to 17,500, claiming that “unforeseen developments in South Africa created an emergency refugee situation.” Since starting his second term in office last year, Donald Trump has repeatedly made false claims that white Afrikaners are racially targeted and fac

stories.title: Louisiana payout cannot erase pain of Ronald Greene death by police – lawyer
stories.summary: Ben Crump says $4.85m police settlement over fatal traffic stop helps to show how ‘truth must always come to light’ “No amount of money can erase” the pain that motorist Ronald Greene’s death at the hands of Louisiana police inflicted on his loved ones, but a $4.85m settlement which the state has agreed to pay his family helps illustrate how “the truth must always come to light”, their attorney has said. Ben Crump recently expressed those sentiments in a statement that served as one of his and h

stories.title: Australians with Down’s syndrome among those to suffer most from proposed NDIS cuts government analysis says
stories.summary: ‘It’s heartless to cut supports we need to leave the house, work and study at a time when the cost of living has dramatically increased’, advocate says Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Proposed funding cuts to NDIS would disadvantage Australians with visual impairment, psychosocial disability and Down’s syndrome the most, according to government analysis, with the government hoping to slash those budgets by 50% by the end of 2027. It also forecasts the cost of the NDI

stories.title: ‘Foolish’ CSIRO job cuts will mean Australia unable to provide climate projections to global reports, scientists warn
stories.summary: Exclusive: Science agency is planning to sack a third of the team working on the national climate model, sources say Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Job cuts at the national science agency mean Australia will no longer be able to submit climate projections to form part of global reports and will have significantly reduced ability to forecast future damage to the country, leading researchers have warned. Multiple sources told Guardian Australia that CSIRO planned to s

stories.title: Minister defends Mandelson file redactions and says documents to be released in June – UK politics live
stories.summary: Darren Jones says release will be ‘one of the largest government publications ever laid in this house’ On Friday parliament’s intelligence and security committee issued a damning statement about the government’s response to the humble address requiring the release of documents relating to the appointment of Peter Mandelson as ambassador to the US. It said the government was not fully complying with what is in effect an instruction from the Commons. For good measure, the committee also accuses th

stories.title: Voters in six states head to polls with Kentucky’s Massie facing Trump’s fury – US politics live
stories.summary: Contest seen as latest test of president’s grip on GOP as he urges voters to reject one of the few senior Republicans who has defied him Sign up for the Breaking News US email The president held an impromptu news conference against the backdrop of the White House’s proposed ballroom’s construction Monday morning. He spoke about the ballroom, Iran, Cuba, the Kentucky congressional race, and the new “reflecting lake”, which he wants to inaugurate before July 4. While showing reporters how the cons

stories.title: Pressure on Mexico after two ex-officials surrender to US over alleged cartel ties
stories.summary: Mexican president Claudia Sheinbaum denies any links between her Morena party and organized crime Pressure is mounting on Claudia Sheinbaum, Mexico’s president, after two former top officials from the country’s Sinaloa state – both members of her Morena party – gave themselves up to US authorities over alleged ties to the Sinaloa cartel. stories.title: From sanctioned cars to beauty clinics, Russian rubles have flowed into China’s border towns since Ukraine war
stories.summary: Suifenhe, a small city in China’s economically depressed rust belt, is a microcosm of an evolving Chinese-Russian trading relationship Suited and booted in a navy twinset tracksuit and colourful high-top trainers, Wang Runguo is hustling. Darting across the gleaming floors of his cavernous car showroom, the 45-year-old from one of China’s poorest provinces is closing on yet another deal. It is all in a day’s work for the man whose salary has more than doubled in the past year thanks to a well-ti

stories.summary: This blog is now closed. stories.title: How China became Russia's economic lifeline
stories.summary: Hot on the heels of Donald Trump's visit, Russian President Vladimir Putin travels to Beijing for talks with Xi Jinping. DW explores how China's trade and financial support is helping keep Russia's economy afloat.

stories.title: Germany news: 3 missing after Görlitz house collapse
stories.summary: Three people are still missing after a house collapsed in the eastern German city of Görlitz on Monday evening, while a study has found that one in five German gas stations have breached pricing laws. DW has more.

stories.title: Spanish ex-PM Zapatero investigated over airline bailout
stories.summary: A Spanish court says it is probing ex-Prime Minister Jose Luis Rodriguez Zapatero for alleged influence peddling. The case puts more pressure on the current premier, Pedro Sanchez, who is dogged by corruption probes.

stories.title: Iran war: Why is Trump blaming the Kurds?
stories.summary: Donald Trump says Kurdish forces kept US weapons meant for Iranian protesters, allegations Kurdish leaders categorically deny. Experts tell DW the president is scapegoating them for his own policy failures.

stories.title: Germany: Major boost for civil defense
stories.summary: Germany's government wants to spend €10 billion to better protect its population in case of emergencies. The backlog of needs is huge.

stories.title: Tasmanian government apologises over stolen body parts scandal
stories.summary: An investigation found that 177 human specimens from dozens of bodies were secretly kept by a museum.

stories.title: Russia maintains heightened intensity as it unleashes another major nighttime barrage at Ukraine
stories.summary: Kyiv says Russia has launched one of its largest barrages at Ukraine in recent days, with the country’s aerial defences reporting more than 500 drones and dozens of missiles fired into Ukraine, targeting various cities.

stories.title: Two dead in Spain shooting, with babies reportedly among injured
stories.summary: Police say a 25-year-old man is suspected of killing his parents and injuring four others in southern Spain.

stories.title: Clashes erupt in Bolivia amid economic crisis
stories.summary: Bolivia faces growing protests, roadblocks and clashes with police amid shortages, inflation and economic unrest.

stories.title: Protest erupts at Venezuela’s Central University after prisoner deaths
stories.summary: Students clash with police at Venezuela's Central University after Carmen Navas dies, renewing anger over her son's death in state custody.

stories.title: US lawmakers raise alarm over legality of Pentagon's lethal drug boat strikes
stories.summary: Both Democratic and Republican lawmakers have questioned whether the military has the legal authority to use lethal force against civilian-style vessels in international waters outside of a declared theatre of war.

stories.title: Liberals open to a third term for Metsola at helm of EU Parliament
stories.summary: STRASBOURG — European Parliament President Roberta Metsola’s chances of securing a third term were given a boost after the liberal Renew group signaled it could support her reelection bid — if its conditions are met. “We support a president candidate who would keep the far-right out of the bureau [the Parliament’s top decision-making body, composed of the president and her 14 deputies] and who is committed to make the centrist political groups’ coalition work,” Renew chie

stories.title: Ex-commando Al Carns ‘keeping options open’ for challenge to Keir Starmer
stories.summary: LONDON — A little-known British defense minister is being urged by MPs to consider a bid to replace Keir Starmer, as a leadership crisis consumes the governing Labour Party. Al Carns, the minister for the armed forces, is being touted by supporters who say the party now needs a radical change if it is to win back thousands of voters who dumped Labour for Nigel Farage’s Reform UK or the left-wing Greens at recent local elections. Carns, who only entered parliament as the MP for the Birmingh

stories.title: Cuba warns US of ‘bloodbath’ if military action follows drone claims
stories.summary: Cuba’s president, Miguel Díaz-Canel, says any US strike would be catastrophic after reports of 300+ drones Cuba’s president, Miguel Díaz-Canel, has warned that any US military action against his country would lead to a “bloodbath” with incalculable consequences for regional peace and stability. “Cuba does not represent a threat,” Díaz-Canel said in a post on X. stories.title: Fears of new China shock as EU industry’s reliance on imports grows
stories.summary: Rising volume of components imported from China prompts warning of cannibalisation of European industries Europe is facing a fresh China shock that threatens to cannibalise local factories, leading to job losses and de facto colonisation of industry by Beijing, trade analysts and representatives have said. stories.title: Calls for release of Sierra Leonean singer jailed in ‘crackdown on free speech’
stories.summary: Zainab Sheriff unjustly sentenced to four years in prison for incitement and threatening language, say activists Lawyers, politicians and activists have called for the release of one of Sierra Leone’s best-known celebrities, who they said was unjustly imprisoned as part of a government crackdown on free speech and political dissent. stories.title: Why more US women are moving abroad: ‘It’s because of Trump, right? Yes and no’
stories.summary: As many as 40% of women aged 15 to 44 say they want to migrate, citing better work life balance to Trump’s politics It was in 2022, when Americans were reeling from the news that the supreme court had overturned Roe v Wade , that Jen Barnett got a firsthand glimpse of just how viable her new business could be. stories.title: Vladimir Putin to arrive in Beijing for state visit hot on heels of Trump
stories.summary: Russian leader says relations between two countries have reached ‘unprecedented level’ on eve of 25th visit to China Vladimir Putin is due to arrive in Beijing on Tuesday for a state visit, four days after Donald Trump left China. stories.title: Experts locate bodies of four missing Italian divers inside Maldives cave
stories.summary: Spokesperson for Indian Ocean island nation says they will try to recover explorers in next couple of days Rescuers have located the bodies of four Italian divers deep inside an underwater cave in an atoll in the Maldives, four days after they were reported missing. stories.summary: Four crew members safely ejected from US Navy jets and are in a stable condition after the collision.

stories.title: Iran has responded to latest US peace proposal, foreign ministry official says
stories.summary: On Sunday, Iran's Fars news agency said Washington had presented a five-point list, which included a demand for Iran to keep only one nuclear site in operation and transfer its stockpile of highly enriched uranium to the US.

stories.title: Taiwan’s president defends US arms purchases that Trump called 'bargaining chip'
stories.summary: Trump’s calling of arms sales to Taiwan a “bargaining chip” has raised concerns on the self-governing island of a potential change in US foreign policy towards the territory after the US president’s latest visit to China.

stories.title: North Korea's Kim calls on military to bolster frontline defences against 'arch enemy'
stories.summary: The plans come at a low point in relations with South Korea and Pyongyang unresponsive to Seoul's repeated offers for dialogue.

stories.title: Watch: Bulgaria welcomes home Eurovision winner Dara with trophy
stories.summary: Eurovision winner Dara returned to Sofia to cheering fans after securing Bulgaria's first-ever victory at the song contest in Vienna.

stories.title: Japan arrests US nationals after one entered enclosure at viral baby monkey Punch's zoo
stories.summary: The baby monkey became an internet star this year after the zoo posted photos of Punch clutching an IKEA plush orangutan for comfort after being rejected by his mother.

stories.title: Bodybuilders show off muscles at Southeast Asian championships
stories.summary: Myanmar hosts the Southeast Asian Bodybuilding Championships in Yangon, with athletes from eight countries competing from May 14 to 18.

stories.title: Cats in costumes and beauty contests draw crowds at expo
stories.summary: Thousands of cat lovers gathered at Cat Expo India in Mumbai, where visitors explored cat beauty contests, pet care products, grooming services, accessories and premium food brands alongside dozens of felines.

stories.title: Two men arrested over stunt at enclosure of famous monkey Punch
stories.summary: Two US nationals have been arrested after one jumped the fence of the monkey, who went viral online, police say.

stories.title: Moment two fighter jets collide mid-air during US air show
stories.summary: Two fighter jets collided mid-air during an air show in Idaho, sending both aircraft crashing to the ground as crew members parachuted to safety.

stories.title: Iran sets up Hormuz transit authority to charge ships for passage
stories.summary: Tehran has launched a formal body to manage and charge ships for transit through the Strait of Hormuz in a highly-contested move as tankers pile up in the waterway and peace talks remain deadlocked.

stories.title: Tiger escapes enclosure near Leipzig, one person injured
stories.summary: An escaped tiger has injured a 73-year-old man near Leipzig, with police saying they shot the animal. German media reported the tiger was owned by a controversial performer.

stories.title: ‘Feels like an illusion’: inside post-Maduro Venezuela’s bewildering new era
stories.summary: Four months after Trump’s surprise raid, a political thaw has descended – but mingled with hope is trepidation for what comes next When Ángel Linares heard a strange buzz followed by an explosion, his first thought was that neighbours were setting off fireworks to celebrate the new year. Then his windows shattered, the building’s walls shook and its facade was ripped off, sending him flying on to the ground of an apartment suddenly reduced to rubble. His 85-year-old mother, Jesucita, feared Vene

stories.title: Bulgaria wins Eurovision song contest with Dara's Bangaranga – video
stories.summary: Bulgarian singer's party anthem triumphs at the grand final of the 70th Eurovision song contest in Vienna, giving the country its first win the competition. The Israeli contestant, Noam Bettan, came second in a competition clouded by protests over the country's participation Continue reading...

stories.title: Top of Labour in row over EU as Nandy challenges Streeting’s remarks
stories.summary: Ex-health secretary’s allies rally to defend him after culture secretary calls his comments about rejoining bloc ‘odd’ A row has broken out at the top of the Labour party over whether Britain should try to rejoin the EU after Wes Streeting said the country should eventually seek to regain membership. Streeting, who resigned as health secretary on Thursday in protest at Keir Starmer’s leadership, kicked off a war of words after he argued on Saturday that Britain’s future lay back in the EU. Conti

stories.title: Germany stakes claim in new space race
stories.summary: German companies of all sizes and all over the country are developing satellites and space applications. They want to be part of the lucrative industry, which is increasingly crucial for civilian and military purposes.

stories.title: Germany news: UNICEF says child well-being ranking 'alarming'
stories.summary: A new study ranks Germany near the bottom in child well-being and education. Meanwhile, an escaped tiger was killed by police after injuring a man near Leipzig. DW has more.

stories.title: Dead humpback whale off Denmark is 'Timmy'
stories.summary: Divers have confirmed that a dead humpback whale spotted off Denmark is the same creature that spent weeks beached off Germany's Baltic coast. It comes two weeks after a contentious rescue operation.

stories.title: German singer Heino pushes back against the far-right AfD
stories.summary: A far‑right politician in Germany used schlager icon Heino in election advertising without his consent. Heino is now demanding compensation for pain and suffering.

stories.title: London on edge as Tommy Robinson backers and pro-Palestinian marchers rally
stories.summary: Tens of thousands of people joined rival London marches for Tommy Robinson and Palestine, as 4,000 officers used strict routes to avoid clashes and keep order.

stories.title: South Korea: Thousands watch robot monks march through Seoul’s historic streets
stories.summary: Four robot monks in traditional robes joined Seoul’s lantern parade ahead of Buddha’s birthday, symbolising Buddhism’s tech-savvy modernisation.

stories.title: Eurovision final in Vienna overshadowed by protests over Israel’s participation
stories.summary: Hundreds protested in Vienna against Israel’s participation in Eurovision 2026 as security was tightened around the Wiener Stadthalle before the grand final, amid tensions linked to the war in Gaza.

stories.title: Lights, camera, Bangaranga: Highlights from Eurovision
stories.summary: Bulgarian singer Dara stormed to victory in the contest, while Ukraine broke the record for the longest note ever sung at Eurovision.

stories.title: Ebola outbreak is a global health emergency, WHO says
stories.summary: The World Health Organization said that it could be a "much larger" outbreak than what is currently being identified and reported but that is does not meet the criteria of a pandemic emergency.

stories.title: US reportedly dropped fraud charges against Indian billionaire after he hired Trump’s lawyer
stories.summary: Gautam Adani, richest man in Asia, was accused of conspiring to pay $250m in bribes to Indian government officials The US Department of Justice is dropping its fraud charges against the Indian billionaire Gautam Adani, the richest man in Asia, after he hired a new legal team led by Donald Trump’s personal lawyer, according to new reports. In an undisclosed April meeting at the justice department, Trump’s personal lawyer, Robert J Giuffra Jr, said that Adani would invest $10bn in the US economy a

stories.title: German intelligence offices snub US-based Palantir software
stories.summary: Germany's domestic intelligence agency has reportedly chosen a data analysis system from France, instead of US-based Palantir. Civil society has welcomed the move, but says more must be done.
